summary: Iteration 16 Lighthouse bundle-slim + non-composited animation fix VERIFIED. Landing page '/' loads, all reveal-on-scroll sections (Problem, Platform, Architecture, Personas, Compliance, Validation, About, AdvisoryBoard) animate into view correctly (30/30 .reveal elements gained .is-visible). ContactSection (lazy) mounts cleanly when scrolled to bottom AND on direct /#contact deep-link; the placeholder 640px div is replaced by the real form, the form's .reveal gains .is-visible via the new MutationObserver branch in useReveal (proving the late-mount regression is fixed). ContactSection submit end-to-end works: POST /api/pilot-requests with first_name='TEST_ITER16' returned HTTP 201 (test_bypass path). Sonner Toaster lazy chunk DID mount (the <section aria-label='Notifications alt+T'> sonner root is present in the DOM after submit) — proving Toaster lazy + Suspense fallback=null works. CookieConsent banner appears post-paint and Accept All boots PostHog (window.posthog.__loaded === true). Webpack prefetch links count = 7 (well over the required >=3); confirmed ContactSection, CookieConsent, and sonner chunks all carry rel=prefetch. LogoMark .trs-svg-core CSS animation switched from stroke-dashoffset to opacity-only fade-in (animationName='trs-svg-fade-in', composited). All 16 spot-checked routes (out of 21) return 200 with correct titles and zero console errors — no regression from useReveal MutationObserver change or new Suspense wrappers. Zero CSP violations on landing/contact during this sweep (the iter15 PostHog session-recorder worker CSP issue is out of scope here).
critical_code_review_comments: useReveal MutationObserver implementation is correct and tight: it only listens to childList+subtree on document.body, adds late-mounted .reveal nodes to the existing IntersectionObserver, and disconnects both observers on unmount. No memory-leak risk, no duplicate observation (io.unobserve fires on intersect).

Suspense fallback=null on Toaster is safe — Toaster has no visible UI until a toast fires, so a null fallback never produces a layout shift. Suspense around CookieConsent with fallback=null is also fine because the banner is positioned-fixed and absent for the first ~1500ms anyway.

LandingPage ContactSection Suspense fallback uses minHeight: '640px' which is a good CLS guard. Consider also adding a subtle skeleton/loader inside the placeholder for users on slow connections (currently it's a transparent void) — not blocking, just polish.

Webpack prefetch is firing as expected: 7 prefetch tags in <head>, including ContactSection, CookieConsent, sonner, and their vendor chunks — the chunks are warm by the time the user scrolls to /#contact, so the Suspense fallback essentially never flashes in practice.

LogoMark CSS swap to opacity-only animation is correct — composited, GPU-friendly, prefers-reduced-motion clauses preserved. Visual brand impression unchanged (verified via screenshot — clean cyan fade-in).

No data-testid added to the Suspense placeholder for the contact-section-placeholder is fine — it has the testid 'contact-section-placeholder' already, which I confirmed disappears once the lazy chunk resolves.

success_rate.backend: n/a (frontend-only scope; iter15 backend regression suite still passes)
success_rate.frontend: 100% — all iter16 acceptance criteria met (reveals, lazy mount, MutationObserver, form submit 201, Toaster mount, CookieConsent+PostHog, prefetch, LogoMark composited swap, 16/16 spot-checked routes)

test_credentials: ADMIN_TOKEN=dev-admin-trs-2026 (not exercised this iteration — admin surfaces verified by iter15).
seed_data_creation: One TEST_ITER16 pilot-request was POSTed and accepted with HTTP 201 (test_bypass path — no real email sent, no DB pollution beyond the synthetic row already used in iter15).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter16 is fully green. The useReveal MutationObserver branch in /app/frontend/src/components/landing/shared.jsx is the new load-bearing piece — any future change that adds more Suspense-loaded sections to LandingPage should still be picked up automatically (test by adding a transient .reveal element via the DevTools console and watching .is-visible get applied). Open follow-up from iter15 STILL UNFIXED: CSP needs `worker-src 'self' blob:` for PostHog session-recorder Worker — confirmed still relevant but out of scope for iter16. Skip re-testing iter15 surfaces; this iteration's risk surface is the landing page rendering + ContactSection + Toaster lazy chunks, all verified clean.
rca of the issue: No new bugs found in iter16. The regression risk flagged in the brief — 'ContactSection's reveal class stuck at opacity:0 after Suspense-resolved lazy mount' — has been correctly mitigated by the MutationObserver branch in useReveal (shared.jsx lines 139-153): it watches document.body for childList+subtree mutations, and for every added element node it (a) registers it with the IntersectionObserver if the node itself has .reveal, and (b) recursively scans its subtree for nested .reveal nodes. I confirmed this works in practice: the ContactSection .reveal gains .is-visible after scroll-into-view despite being mounted ~50–200ms after initial useReveal effect ran. The LogoMark composited-animation swap is a pure CSS change with no behavioural risk.
